section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain to palpation of the right lumbar spine.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Pain to palpation is an examination finding elicited by the clinician, not a typical patient-reported symptom description.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports decreased flexion and extension of the back.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Decreased range of motion is usually documented as an exam finding; phrased as a patient report it is somewhat acceptable in HPI but more appropriate under Findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports a positive straight leg raise.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A positive straight leg raise is a specific physical exam maneuver result and should be documented as a finding, not as a patient-reported symptom.

section_miscategorizations.text: Examination reveals pain in the left hand.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This introduces a new symptom (left hand pain) that is not part of the documented chief complaint or back-pain-focused exam; without context it reads more like an additional complaint/history item than a focused finding for this visit.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has been taking ibuprofen.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Current medication use belongs in the history/medication context, not in the exam/labs/imaging Findings section.
